prompt: I need to write a UART driver for a custom board with an ARM Cortex-M4 MCU (not using HAL). The UART peripheral is at base address 0x40004400. I need to implement init (115200 baud, 8N1), blocking send byte, and interrupt-driven receive into a buffer. PCLK is 84MHz. Write it in C99.

assertions.name: volatile_registers
assertions.description: All hardware register accesses use volatile

assertions.name: correct_baud_calculation
assertions.description: Baud rate divisor calculation is correct for 84MHz PCLK and 115200 baud

assertions.name: ring_buffer_for_rx
assertions.description: Uses a ring buffer for interrupt-driven receive

assertions.name: c99_compliance
assertions.description: Uses C99 style: fixed-width types, designated initializers, no C++ features

assertions.name: isr_reads_dr
assertions.description: ISR reads the data register to clear the RXNE flag

assertions.name: static_assert_buffer_size
assertions.description: Uses _Static_assert to validate buffer size (e.g., power of 2) at compile time

assertions.name: documents_baud_error
assertions.description: Documents or calculates the baud rate error percentage

assertions.name: explains_rxne_clear_mechanism
assertions.description: Explains WHY reading DR clears RXNE (hardware auto-clear on read)
